pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 20 creates JSON file at this exact path
export_result.sh line 10 defines OUTPUT_FILE variable; verifier.py expects this file
verifier.py line 82 checks h1_count >= 6; input data supports 6+ major sections (Posts, Patrol, Access Control, Emergency Procedures, Contacts, Shifts, plus cover/intro sections)
verifier.py line 92 checks h2_count >= 10; input data provides 34+ potential subsection items (4 posts + 8 patrol checkpoints + 5 access levels + 4 emergency types + 10 contacts + 3 shifts)

verifier.py line 103 checks table_count >= 5; input JSON structure provides exactly 5 natural table sources: Posts (4 rows), Patrol Checkpoints (8 rows), Access Levels (5 rows), Contacts (10 rows), Shift Schedule (3 rows)
setup_task.sh line 23 defines company.name = 'Sentinel Shield Security Services, LLC'; export_result.sh line 82 checks for this string in document
setup_task.sh line 29 defines client.name = 'Helios BioSciences, Inc.'; export_result.sh line 83 checks for this string in document
setup_task.sh line 38 defines document.document_number = 'SPO-HBC-2024-003'; also used as output filename and verified by export_result.sh
task.json description mentions 'auto-generated Table of Contents'; export_result.sh line 72 checks for 'text:table-of-content' in content.xml; verifier.py line 74 rewards presence
task.json description mentions 'page numbers in the footer'; export_result.sh line 98 checks for footer style definitions (<style:footer or <text:footer); verifier.py line 114 checks page_numbers

privileged_info_summary: This task creates a Security Post Orders (SPO) document from synthetic security operations data. The input file is JSON-formatted test data generated at /home/ga/Documents/site_security_data.json containing realistic but fabricated security operations information for a fictional pharmaceutical research campus (Helios BioSciences). The output document (SPO-HBC-2024-003.odt) must contain at least 6 primary sections (Heading 1), 10 subsections (Heading 2), and 5 data tables. The document must include a Table of Contents, footer with page numbers, and incorporate the required security company name (Sentinel Shield), client name (Helios BioSciences), and document identifier (SPO-HBC-2024-003). All metadata claims about document structure requirements are appropriately calibrated to the available input data: 5 natural table sources exist (Posts, Patrol Checkpoints, Access Levels, Contacts, Shifts), and 34+ potential subsection items can support the Heading 2 minimum of 10.
